recent_calls.summary: The user called Budget Heating and Plumbing to schedule a service appointment for their air conditioning unit, which has a frozen A coil and is not cooling. The agent collected the user's details and preferred appointment time before transferring the call to a live agent for further assistance.
The user called and requested to speak to Amy. The agent acknowledged the request and successfully transferred the call to the appropriate live agent.
The user requested to speak with Sigi, and the agent transferred the call to the appropriate person successfully. The call lasted 24 seconds and ended with a call transfer.
Andrew from American Home Shield called and requested to speak with a live agent. The call was promptly transferred to the appropriate live agent without any issues.
Andrew from American Home Shield called and requested to speak with a concierge agent. The agent, Sophie, transferred the call to a live agent successfully.

The call was very brief and the user mentioned being in an appointment, indicating they were not available to talk. The agent attempted to engage but the user did not provide further information before hanging up.

The user called about an air conditioning issue that was not working and indicated it was an emergency. The agent attempted to schedule a service appointment but the user requested to speak with a live agent. The call was then transferred to another agent for further assistance.

The user called and requested to speak with a live agent regarding billing and air conditioning. The agent apologized for not having direct access to billing information and initiated a transfer to the billing team for further assistance. The call ended after the transfer was initiated.

Ken Siebold called to report that his whole house air conditioning unit was not cooling due to a likely low Freon issue. He provided his details and preferred a service appointment on Monday, preferably in the morning or afternoon. The agent confirmed the information and informed Ken that someone from Budget Heating, Cooling & Plumbing would contact him shortly; Ken declined information about maintenance programs and feedback.

Courtney called to request a service appointment for her air conditioner, which runs but does not blow cool air. The agent confirmed the details, including the home warranty dispatch number and preferred appointment days, and informed Courtney that someone would contact her shortly. Courtney declined information about maintenance programs and feedback requests.

The user called to inquire about a service appointment for heating and cooling at their house and wanted to speak to a live representative. The agent attempted to connect the user to a live agent for further assistance.

The user called to inquire about a technician named Cole who left due to needing parts and did not provide a return time. The agent apologized for the inconvenience and transferred the call to someone who could provide live updates.

The user, a home warranty representative, called to inquire about reopening a canceled air conditioning service dispatch for a customer. The agent confirmed that the customer can call to schedule the appointment and that the work order can be reopened. The user decided to have the customer call back to schedule the service.

The user, Ruth, called to inquire about scheduling an appointment for a central air unit installation with financing options. The agent could not find an existing appointment and offered to leave a message or transfer to a live representative. The user requested a transfer, and the call was successfully transferred to a live agent.

The user called to inquire about rescheduling a dispatch for their air conditioning unit that is not cooling. The agent collected the service address, contact number, and preferred service window, confirming the details with the user. The user declined to provide feedback, and the agent explained that the team will review the request and confirm the appointment.

The user called Budget Heating, Cooling and Plumbing after receiving a call about replacing their air conditioner. The user expressed urgency to speak with someone knowledgeable, but became frustrated with the initial agent and requested to be connected to a live agent. The call was then transferred successfully to another agent.
The call was very brief and disconnected abruptly after the agent's initial greeting and request for service information.

The user called to request a cancellation of service and provided their address, name, and role as the property owner. The agent gathered necessary information but the call ended abruptly before completion.
The user requested to speak to a live representative, and the agent promptly transferred the call to a live agent. The transfer was successful, and the call ended shortly after.

Azaria from American Home Shield called to inquire about the earliest available date for a work order, specifically if the 27th was the soonest. The agent explained that home warranty service requests follow a different process and cannot be booked through this line, but assured that the request would be forwarded to the appropriate team for follow-up.

The user called to discuss scheduling an appointment related to American Home Shield and needed to speak with a live person. The agent attempted to assist but ultimately transferred the call to a live agent for further help.
The user called and requested to speak with someone directly. The agent promptly initiated a transfer to a live agent to assist the user further.

The user called to follow up on a callback regarding scheduling an appointment to fix their water heater. The agent acknowledged the issue and transferred the call to a live agent for further assistance.

The user, Chris Ziegler, called to set up a service appointment and provided personal and service address details. The user requested to speak with a live person, and the agent transferred the call to a human representative. The call ended with a successful transfer to a live agent.

The user, Douglas Stewart, reported an issue with his central air conditioner making a popping sound and emitting smoke from the compressor. The agent collected all necessary information, including the American Home Shield dispatch number, and scheduled a service appointment preferably for the next day. The user declined information about maintenance programs and ended the call after confirming the details.
The call was very brief with the user not providing any audible response. The agent greeted the user but the call ended almost immediately after.

The user, Chris Ziegler, called to report a leaking hot water heater and requested an estimate for replacement. The agent collected all necessary information and offered available appointment slots, with the user selecting Wednesday, April 22nd. The call ended abruptly after the user asked if the appointment was being set up and did not receive a response.

Frank from Hemlane called to issue a payment for a completed hot water tank repair at a property in Saint Louis, Missouri. The agent confirmed the details and contact information, and Frank declined additional information about maintenance programs. The call ended positively with Frank providing excellent feedback on the service.

The user, Loretha Haf, called to schedule a service appointment for their air conditioning which is not cooling properly. The agent confirmed the details and informed the user that the team would contact them soon to confirm availability for a service around noon today. The user declined information about maintenance programs and provided positive feedback about the call experience.
The user requested to speak to a customer service representative, and the agent promptly transferred the call to a live agent. The transfer was successful, and the call ended shortly after.
The user called customer service and was promptly transferred to a live agent for further assistance. The call lasted 15 seconds and ended with a successful transfer.
The agent attempted to engage the user by asking for their service address ZIP code, but the user did not respond and hung up shortly after.
The user returned a call and requested someone to come back for assistance. The agent acknowledged the request and transferred the call to a live agent for further help.

The user called back after receiving a text and expressed interest in setting up an appointment for an IDP check. The agent attempted to clarify if the request was related to a home warranty plan but the call ended before the user could provide more details.

The user called to schedule a service appointment for a lack of hot water, referencing a claim filed with American Home Shield. The agent gathered details about the issue and preferred appointment times, but the call ended abruptly before the dispatch number was provided or the appointment was scheduled.

The user called to check on a service request for their home warranty regarding their AC unit. They provided the dispatch number and requested a regular service appointment for the afternoon. The agent gathered necessary details to pass along the request for scheduling.

John from American Home Shield called to request the earliest possible appointment for a customer with a geothermal system that is not cooling. The agent confirmed the details and assured that Budget Heating, Cooling & Plumbing would follow up with the customer and notify them about the nearest available appointment. The user also agreed to receive information about home maintenance programs.

The user called to inquire about the details of their current system, specifically the model and serial number. The agent informed the user that the request would be passed to the team for follow-up. The call ended with the user satisfied and no further questions.

The call was a brief inbound conversation where the user, Nick from ADP, greeted the agent Sophie, who introduced herself and asked how she could assist. The call ended shortly after without further discussion or resolution.

The user called to schedule a service appointment for their air conditioning units, one needing maintenance and the other not cooling properly. The agent confirmed the details and booked an appointment for Monday, April 27th at 2 PM. The user provided positive feedback, rating the experience a four out of five.
The user called Budget and was quickly transferred to a live agent for further assistance. The initial agent collected minimal information before initiating the transfer.
The user called to speak with a representative about their air conditioning. The agent confirmed it was not an emergency and then transferred the call to a live agent for further assistance.

Cynthia Montefit called to report that the cooling unit installed by Budget Heating and Cooling is not working properly, with the outside unit's blades not turning and the system failing to cool the house. The agent confirmed the service address and contact details, assured Cynthia that the team would address the issue promptly, and no further questions were raised. The call ended with the agent promising a follow-up.

The call involved JB from American Home Shield confirming an active service appointment for an AC and heating unit not coming on at a specified address. The agent collected all necessary details including dispatch number, service address, contact phone, and email, and confirmed the appointment time. The call ended with the user providing positive feedback and requesting a live agent, after which the call was successfully transferred.

The user, Sarah, called to update information related to a previous inquiry and requested a callback to discuss details further. The agent attempted to gather the service address ZIP code but the user ended the call after leaving a callback number.
The user requested to speak with a human agent, and the AI agent promptly transferred the call to a live representative. The transfer was successful, and the call ended shortly after.

The agent informed the user that they would transfer the call to a live agent for further assistance and successfully completed the transfer. The user acknowledged the initial contact but did not engage further before the transfer.
The user requested to speak to Nicole, and the agent transferred the call to the appropriate live agent successfully. The call ended with a transfer without further conversation.
